resource.description: Do not use blood-based or tissue-based biomarkers to monitor patients receiving neoadjuvant therapy.
resource.name: AvoidBloodAndTissueBiomarkerMonitoring

resource.description: Avoid routine neoadjuvant chemotherapy or anti-HER2 agents outside a clinical trial for small node-negative HER2-positive disease.
resource.name: AvoidNeoadjuvantTherapyForSmallHer2Positive

resource.description: Avoid routine neoadjuvant therapy outside a clinical trial for small node-negative TNBC.
resource.name: AvoidNeoadjuvantTherapyForSmallTnbc

resource.description: Do not use other immunochemical markers, morphologic markers, or genomic profiles to decide whether to pursue neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
resource.name: AvoidOtherMarkers

resource.description: Avoid routine neoadjuvant endocrine therapy outside a clinical trial in premenopausal HR-positive HER2-negative early-stage disease.
resource.name: AvoidPremenopausalNeoadjuvantEndocrineTherapy

resource.description: Do not routinely add immune checkpoint inhibitors to standard neoadjuvant chemotherapy for early-stage TNBC.
resource.name: AvoidRoutineImmuneCheckpointInhibitors

resource.description: Carboplatin may be added as part of a neoadjuvant TNBC regimen after weighing potential benefits and harms.
resource.name: ConsiderCarboplatinForTnbc

resource.description: Pertuzumab may be used with trastuzumab in the neoadjuvant setting for HER2-positive disease.
resource.name: ConsiderPertuzumabWithTrastuzumab

resource.description: Manage the patient through a multidisciplinary care team while planning and delivering neoadjuvant therapy.
resource.name: CoordinateMultidisciplinaryManagement

resource.description: Use regular clinical examination to monitor patients receiving neoadjuvant therapy.
resource.name: MonitorWithClinicalExamination

resource.description: Offer an anthracycline-containing and taxane-containing neoadjuvant regimen for TNBC with clinically node-positive disease and-or at least T1c disease.
resource.name: OfferAnthracyclineTaxaneForTnbc

resource.description: Offer anthracycline and taxane or non-anthracycline regimen in combination with trastuzumab for node-positive or high-risk node-negative HER2-positive disease.
resource.name: OfferHer2DirectedNeoadjuvantRegimen

resource.description: Use neoadjuvant chemotherapy as the treatment of choice for inflammatory breast cancer or unresectable locally advanced disease that may become resectable.
resource.name: OfferNeoadjuvantChemotherapyForAdvancedDisease

resource.description: Use chemotherapy with or without targeted therapy, or endocrine therapy when HR-positive, to reduce the extent of local therapy.
resource.name: OfferNeoadjuvantForDownstaging

resource.description: Offer neoadjuvant systemic therapy to high-risk HER2-positive or triple-negative disease when residual disease findings will change adjuvant therapy.
resource.name: OfferNeoadjuvantForResidualDiseaseGuidedAdjuvantPlanning

resource.description: Use neoadjuvant systemic therapy when delaying surgery is preferable or unavoidable.
resource.name: OfferNeoadjuvantForSurgeryDelay

resource.description: Use neoadjuvant endocrine therapy with an aromatase inhibitor in postmenopausal HR-positive HER2-negative disease to increase locoregional treatment options.
resource.name: OfferPostmenopausalNeoadjuvantEndocrineTherapy

resource.description: Use mammography, ultrasound, or MRI that was most informative at baseline to confirm progression or support surgical planning.
resource.name: UseBaselineInformativeImaging

resource.description: Use endocrine therapy for disease control if there is no intent to proceed to surgery.
resource.name: UseEndocrineTherapyForDiseaseControl

resource.description: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy can substitute for adjuvant chemotherapy in HR-positive HER2-negative disease when surgical pathology or genomic testing is not required for the decision.
resource.name: UseNeoadjuvantChemotherapyWithoutSurgicalData

resource.description: Use pathologic complete response, defined as absence of invasive disease in breast and lymph nodes, to guide clinical decision-making.
resource.name: UsePcrToGuideDecisions

resource.description: Use tumor histology, grade, stage, and ER PR HER2 expression to guide the decision to pursue neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
resource.name: UseStandardTumorFeatures

resource.description: Review inflammatory or locally advanced presentation, residual-disease driven adjuvant planning, downstaging goals, and surgery delay scenarios.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolCandidacyAssessmentStep

resource.description: Determine whether neoadjuvant therapy is indicated and what clinical goal it serves.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolCandidacyPhase

resource.description: Choose trastuzumab-based neoadjuvant therapy for high-risk disease and avoid routine use in small node-negative disease.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolHer2PositivePlanningStep

resource.description: Decide whether chemotherapy can be selected without surgical pathology and whether endocrine downstaging is appropriate.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolHrHer2NegativePlanningStep

resource.description: Monitor the patient during therapy and use response findings to guide ongoing decisions.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolMonitoringPhase

resource.description: Select a neoadjuvant regimen according to biologic subtype and clinical risk.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolSubtypePlanningPhase

resource.description: Choose anthracycline taxane chemotherapy, decide on carboplatin, and avoid routine immune checkpoint inhibitor use or low-stage overtreatment.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolTnbcPlanningStep

resource.description: Use histology, grade, stage, and receptor status to anchor treatment planning and avoid unsupported markers.
resource.name: AscoBreastCancerGuidelineProtocolTumorFeatureReviewStep
